role_title: Staff Technical Program Manager, Mergers & Acquisition

location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 5-10+ years in project/program management specifically in M&A integration
Comp range $176K-$220K base in SF, consistent with Staff-level IC at Lyft
Reports to Head of Central TPM

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5-10+ years in project/program management, specifically in M&A integration, corporate development, or transformation roles.
Deep understanding of back-office operations (Systems/Finance/HR) and M&A life cycle.
Strong leadership, cross-functional influencing skills, and the ability to work in high-stakes, fast-paced environments.
Familiarity with IT systems integration, marketplace infrastructure and associated financial data pipelines.
Lead the back office due diligence assessment for future acquisitions.
Develop and execute comprehensive integration roadmaps, strategies, and playbooks for business functions.

operating across a modern enterprise stack including Workday, Okta, Google Workspace, Oracle Fusion, and Salesforce and knowing how to bring an acquired company into that environment with speed and precision.
Ensure compliance with corporate standards, including SOX controls, identity access management, and security protocols.
Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, or related field; MBA is often preferred.

scope.surface: Enterprise Systems M&A integration — back-office (Finance, HR, IT, Legal, Marketing, Sales, Operations) across acquisition lifecycles
scope.business_model: B2C / B2B rideshare and mobility platform
primary_metrics_inferred: Day 1 and Day 100 integration milestones achieved
Synergy realization / financial and operational ROI vs. targets
Timeline and risk management across integration workstreams
core_responsibilities: Lead back office due diligence assessment for future acquisitions
Develop and execute comprehensive integration roadmaps, strategies, and playbooks for business functions
Coordinate HR, Finance, IT, Legal, Marketing, Sales and Operations teams for timely transition and milestone execution
Identify dependencies, mitigate risks, and resolve issues related to system, process, or team integration
Track financial and operational metrics against targets to ensure value capture and measure ROI
Facilitate governance meetings and provide regular updates to executive leadership
Oversee migration of data, legal entities, and operational processes (payroll, IT infrastructure)
Ensure compliance with SOX controls, identity access management, and security protocols
Lead Day 1 and Day 100 planning for minimal employee impact and business continuity

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($176K-$220K base in SF area)
Clear and specific role scope — M&A integration with defined systems stack
Named enterprise tools (Workday, Okta, Oracle Fusion, Salesforce, Google Workspace) give concrete technical expectations
Hybrid policy clearly stated (3 days/week Mon, Wed, Thu)
Equity and bonus mentioned in addition to base
Reports to Head of Central TPM — clear reporting line
Role narrative is compelling and articulates real employee impact
red_flags: MBA listed as 'often preferred' in the experience section — ambiguous whether it is truly soft or carries hidden weight in evaluation
Hybrid requires in-office 3 days/week in San Francisco — limits candidates outside the Bay Area
Visa sponsorship not addressed — unclear for international candidates
Years range '5-10+' is unusually broad and may indicate uncertainty about level calibration

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, role title 'Staff', and years range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicit in JD)
location.visa_sponsorship (not mentioned in JD; marked unclear)
ambiguities: The years range '5-10+' is unusually wide; it is unclear whether this is a Staff-level role expecting 10+ years or a Senior role expecting 5+. Treated as Staff based on title and comp.
MBA is described as 'often preferred' — this phrasing is non-standard and could be interpreted as stronger than a typical nice-to-have. Classified as soft/nice-to-have here.
Bachelor's degree is listed in the Experience section with no 'required' qualifier, but it reads as a baseline expectation alongside the preferred MBA; classified as hard accordingly.
Compensation note says 'not inclusive of potential equity offering, bonus or benefits' — equity and bonus are mentioned but not quantified.
